element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Mansion hub area with tutorial-adjacent NPC interactions
Narrative context: searching for kidnapped nephew Pubert E. Addams
Integrated with dungeon exploration and NPC dialogue
Culmination of dungeon progression; final boss requires item management (Black Egg trap mechanic)
Side quests and story progression via Addams family members
Item management; reserved slots for Spider's Eye and unused items (Black Egg, Portal Potion)
Petrified wilderness, swamp, sewage areas referenced in debug cutscenes

typical_tension_triggers: Regular enemy spawns during dungeon exploration
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Eight-dungeon structure with hub-based progression; alternates between exploration tension and dialogue relief via Addams family interactions

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Side quests scattered throughout mansion hub and dungeon areas
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden sound test and level select accessible via debug menu suggests designer-intended secrets; debug walk-through-walls feature indicates environmental depth beyond visible paths

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory combat and puzzle mechanics in Mansion setting; NPC guidance available
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Eight dungeons with escalating enemy density and puzzle complexity; boss encounters per dungeon
difficulty_curve.late_game: Final boss encounter with trap mechanics (Black Egg intended as punishment for overconfidence); complex combat and inventory management required
notable_spikes: Final boss room (Morobe's Black Egg trap mechanic)
Transition from hub exploration to dungeon gauntlet

notable_level_patterns.description: Eight explorable dungeons accessible from Mansion hub; player returns to NPCs for dialogue/quests between dungeon runs
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Petrified Wilderness, Swamp, Sewage areas (inferred from debug cutscene labels)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each dungeon culminates in a boss fight; final boss designed as trap encounter (Black Egg explosion) to subvert player expectations and create memorable failure state
Dungeons named after thematic Addams Family locations: Pumpkin, Wilderness, Swamp, Sewage, Mansion transitions, Gargoyle areas
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Wednesday's Pumpkin Patch, Foul Sewage dungeon

notable_level_patterns.description: NPC interactions (Addams family members) provide story exposition and likely quest direction; Thing character appears as narrative beat (cutscene: Thing escapes up vine)

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor) - Debug features, cutscene list, unused items documentation
wikipedia - Gameplay overview, eight dungeons confirmation, character role clarification
